skill.name: backend-fundamentals
skill.description: Reviews backend API routes, server logic, middleware, and service layers for REST design, validation, error handling, and separation of concerns.
skill.summary: Review backend code for API design, security basics, and maintainable server architecture.
skill.author: DanielPodolsky

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged external command and weak cryptography patterns, but review found these are false positives from markdown fences, API terminology, status-code text, and illustrative JavaScript examples. No executable scripts, network calls, filesystem operations, credential access, data exfiltration, or prompt injection attempts were found in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: False Positive: External Command Detection in Markdown Examples
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged Ruby or shell backtick execution, but the referenced lines are markdown code fences, route examples, a SQL-injection anti-pattern example, and an architecture diagram. These are instructional examples inside SKILL.md, not executable skill code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text is contained in documentation and fenced examples. No instruction asks the model to run shell commands or execute the example content.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Detection in Non-Crypto Text
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged weak cryptography patterns at description, API design, and status-code lines. Review found no cryptographic algorithm, hashing instruction, encryption implementation, or security bypass guidance at these locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines contain ordinary backend review wording such as API design and status codes. There is no semantic evidence of MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or similar weak cryptography usage.

content.value_statement: Backend reviews often miss contract design, validation, and error behavior. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through structured API and server architecture review.
actual_capabilities: Reviews REST route design, naming, versioning, and HTTP status code usage.
Checks separation between routes, controllers, services, repositories, and models.
Prompts review of input validation, authentication, authorization, and rate limiting.
Highlights common backend anti-patterns such as fat route handlers and leaked internal errors.
Provides Socratic questions for mentoring junior developers during backend reviews.
Includes a compact reference for common HTTP status codes and backend layers.
limitations: Does not execute tests, run linters, or inspect a live application by itself.
Focuses on general backend fundamentals rather than framework-specific implementation details.
Does not provide a complete security audit for production systems.
Uses examples centered on JavaScript and Node.js style backend code.

use_cases.title: Review an API Pull Request
use_cases.description: Use the checklist to assess route design, validation, errors, and layer boundaries before merging backend changes.

use_cases.title: Mentor a Junior Developer
use_cases.description: Use the Socratic questions to help a developer reason about backend design without giving every answer directly.

use_cases.title: Refactor Server Logic
use_cases.description: Identify fat routes, mixed data access, and unclear responsibilities before moving code into cleaner layers.

prompt_templates.title: Basic API Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this backend route using the backend fundamentals checklist. Focus on REST naming, status codes, validation, and error responses.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a single route handler.

prompt_templates.title: Middleware and Auth Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these API routes for middleware order, authentication, authorization, input validation, and rate limiting gaps.

prompt_templates.title: Architecture Layer Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this backend module for separation of concerns across routes, controllers, services, repositories, and models.

prompt_templates.title: Mentoring Review Session
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Socratic questions to guide a junior developer through the backend issues in this pull request. Prioritize questions by risk.

output_examples.input: A route handler validates input, performs database access, sends email, and returns the response in one large function.
output_examples.output: The review identifies a fat route, recommends moving business logic into a service, and suggests keeping the route focused on HTTP concerns.

output_examples.input: A protected endpoint reads a user identifier from the request body and returns matching records.
output_examples.output: The review asks how ownership is verified, whether authentication is required, and how unexpected fields are rejected.

output_examples.input: An endpoint returns a success status while the body contains an error message.
output_examples.output: The review flags the status-code mismatch and recommends returning a client or not-found error that matches the failure.

best_practices: Use the checklist during pull requests before discussing style or formatting concerns.
Pair findings with concrete risk, such as data leaks, broken clients, or hard-to-test code.
Ask the Socratic questions when mentoring instead of rewriting the developer's solution first.
anti_patterns: Do not treat the checklist as a substitute for tests, threat modeling, or production security review.
Do not apply REST conventions mechanically when the product has documented API standards.
Do not ignore framework-specific behavior for errors, middleware order, or request parsing.

faq.question: What does this skill review?
faq.answer: It reviews backend routes, middleware, server logic, API contracts, validation, error handling, and layer separation.

faq.question: Which tools can use it?
faq.answer: It is packaged for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows.

faq.question: Does it run backend tests?
faq.answer: No. It provides review guidance and questions, but it does not execute tests or commands.

faq.question: Is it only for Express applications?
faq.answer: No. The examples use Express-style code, but the review concepts apply to many backend frameworks.

faq.question: Can it replace a security audit?
faq.answer: No. It covers basic backend security checks, but production systems still need dedicated security review.

faq.question: How should reviewers use it?
faq.answer: Use it to structure comments, find high-impact backend issues, and guide developers with focused questions.
